verdict: 2026-07-01 14:06:58 PDT — Shape is mostly sound: the workflows match the approved minimal local gate plus CI layering, permissions are minimal, checkout uses full history, the PR gitleaks range is the right base..head shape, and the two-phase protection plan is coherent if Phase 2 uses the observed check name and explicit status-check payload already recorded in the story. I checked current runner assumptions against GitHub docs: ubuntu-24.04 lists jq and shellcheck (https://github.com/actions/runner-images/blob/main/images/ubuntu/Ubuntu2404-Readme.md), and hosted Linux runners use passwordless sudo for privileged installs (https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/runners/github-hosted-runners). One CI-shape footgun remains.

findings.title: Gitleaks install is global and runs when the scan is skipped
findings.claim: The checksum-pinned download is the right security shape, but the workflow installs gitleaks into /usr/local/bin and does that unconditionally even though the gitleaks scan itself only runs on pull_request. That makes the required push-to-main CI path depend on a global runner write and an external download that produces no scan on that event, so main can go red from a PR-only tool setup rather than from the gate, shellcheck, or an actual secret finding.
findings.alternative: Install gitleaks into a job-local directory such as $RUNNER_TEMP/bin and add it through GITHUB_PATH, or invoke the extracted binary directly. In ci.yml, make install+scan one pull_request-scoped block; if push-to-main scanning is desired, add an explicit before..after push diff scan instead of leaving a skipped scan behind an unconditional install.
findings.win: Eliminates the runner global-path/sudo assumption, removes an unnecessary network failure path from push-to-main CI, and keeps the required check focused on checks that actually run for that event.
